stories.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
stories.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.

stories.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
stories.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.

stories.title: Fear of Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz could further slow the flow of oil
stories.description: Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.

stories.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
stories.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.

stories.title: Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz as U.S.-Israel strikes continue
stories.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."

stories.title: Iran says its new leader made his 1st address, vowing to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
stories.description: Iran's state media issued what it said was a statement by Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, vowing to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and keep up attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.

stories.title: The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis
stories.description: The latest on the Iran war and the growing oil crisis across the Middle East from Dubai, Iraq, Lebanon and Israel.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
stories.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
stories.description: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

stories.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
stories.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
stories.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
stories.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

stories.title: Iran steps up attacks on energy targets as tankers hit
stories.description: Iranian attacks are reported in Bahrain and Oman as two foreign fuel tankers are hit near Iraq.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: 'Not appropriate' for Iran to be at World Cup - Trump
stories.description: US president Donald Trump says Iran are "welcome" at the 2026 World Cup but it is not "appropriate" for them to be there.

stories.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
stories.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

stories.title: Explosions reported on two foreign vessels in the Gulf, authorities say
stories.description: Pressure has intensified on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping lanes, since Israel and the US attacked Iran last month.

stories.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
stories.description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

stories.title: Why air strikes on Tehran oil facilities are causing black rain
stories.description: Air strikes have damaged at least four oil facilities since US-Israeli attacks on Iran began last month.

stories.title: 'I just want to be able to sleep': Attacks in Iran rock cities and cut power
stories.description: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran-linked Iraq group says French interests in region are targets
stories.description: A pro-Iranian group in Iraq warned Friday that French interests "in Iraq and the region" would be "under targeting fire" after the arrival of a French aircraft carrier. The statement on the Telegram page of the Ashab Alkahf group came as French President Emmanuel Macron announced...

stories.title: US allows sale of Russian oil at sea as Middle East war sends energy prices soaring
stories.description: The United States will temporarily allow the sale of Russian oil already at sea, the Treasury Department said on Thursday, as energy prices surged after US-Israeli strikes on Iran triggered war in the Middle East. The license permits cargoes loaded by March 12 to be delivered and...

stories.title: Netanyahu says new Iran leader 'can't show his face in public'
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says that Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei "can't show his face in public" after Tehran issued a first statement in his name that was read out by a TV presenter. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

stories.title: Iran to allow India-flagged tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz (reports)
stories.description: Iran will reportedly allow Indian-flagged tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for 40% of the South Asian nation's crude imports, an Indian government source told Reuters on Thursday, but an Iranian source outside the country denied any such ​deal was reached. FRANC...

stories.title: Iran trolls Trump with AI-generated Lego propaganda video
US Democrats call for 'swift investigation' into strike on Iranian school
stories.description: A group of 46 mostly Democratic senators on Wednesday sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth seeking answers on the bombing of a school in Iran. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports.

stories.title: Oil prices top $100 as market braces for long conflict
stories.description: Brent crude oil prices have once again topped $100 per barrel, as energy markets brace for the Iran conflict to drag on. US President Donald Trump says he's less concerned about high oil prices than about stopping Iran, while his energy secretary has confirmed the military cannot...

stories.title: US tech giants on Iran's list of potential targets in Gulf countries
stories.description: Iran's Tasnim news agency has published a list of potential targets for Iran on Telegram, including the offices of tech giants such as Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Nvidia in Gulf countries and Israel. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24.

stories.title: Who gives the kill order? AI and the war in Iran
stories.description: Warfare's always evolving, sometimes slowly: Napoleon’s armies traveled only as those of Julius Caesar. Sometimes, faster than commanders can process: see the futile carnage of World War I. The pace of change on the battlefield today? Fast, very fast.

stories.title: Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations
stories.description: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warnin...

stories.title: Iran's supreme leader issues first statement since his appointment
stories.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first statement since his appointment as the war with the United States and Israel rages. The statement was read on state television Thursday by a news anchor. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas He...

stories.title: Middle East war: Dubai targeted by fresh Iranian drone strikes
stories.description: #Dubai was among the targets of fresh Iranian drone strikes early on March 12, alongside Kuwait's airport. This comes after #Iran denounced a #UN Security Council resolution that demanded strikes on the #Gulf countries stop. Eleven civilians have died since the start of Iranian a...

stories.title: Oil stays above $100 a barrel amid Iran’s stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Energy markets remain on tenterhooks as the prospect of prolonged war in the Middle East grows.

stories.title: Iranian missile strikes residential area in northern Israel
stories.description: Video from northern Israel captured the moment an Iranian missile impacted in a residential area early Friday morning.

stories.title: Thousands rally in Athens protesting US-Israeli attacks on Iran
stories.description: Protesters in Athens have marched to the US Embassy to condemn the US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed Iranian nuclear scientists
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said several Iranian nuclear scientists were killed in Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump says war going ‘well’ as Gulf under wave of attacks
stories.description: Israeli strikes kill dozens more in Lebanon, including 5 children among nine people killed in attack on village of Arki.

stories.title: Iran envoy says Tehran will keep Strait of Hormuz open
stories.description: Iran’s UN envoy Amir Saeid Iravani said Tehran will not close the Strait of Hormuz and remains committed to freedom.

stories.title: Qatar rejects attempts to ‘drive wedge’ with US over gas pause
stories.description: Qatari official denies political motives for decision to halt LNG production following Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Air attacks target bases of Iran-backed forces in Iraq
stories.description: Air attacks have targeted military bases across Iraq belonging to Iran-backed forces.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel ‘stronger than ever’ in first speech since Iran war
stories.description: Prime minister says US-Israeli war prevented Iran from moving its nuclear and ballistic projects underground.

stories.title: Fuel shortages bring queues, protests around the world
stories.description: Rising fuel prices have caused long queues and protests around the world as the US-Israeli war on Iran rages.

stories.title: How will the war on Iran impact the US economy?
stories.description: Outcomes depend on how quickly oil tankers can return to the Strait of Hormuz, experts said.

stories.title: Does President Trump have an exit strategy for the war with Iran?
stories.description: United States officials are issuing contradictory statements on the war's timeline and objectives.

stories.title: Iran vows to make Trump pay for ‘grave miscalculation’ if US escalates war
stories.description: Ali Larijani responds to US threat to hit Iran's power infrastructure, saying the 'whole region will go dark'.

stories.title: How oil is at the center of the US-Israel war with Iran
stories.description: As oil prices rise, the US, Israel and Iran seem ready to keep fighting no matter the costs.

stories.title: Question of lifting anti-Russian sanctions raised during Japan’s ruling party meeting
stories.description: Muneo Suzuki noted that Japan could use its geographical advantage to replace oil and gas supplies from the Middle East

stories.title: US sees no evidence of Russia helping Iran — envoy to NATO
stories.description: Matthew Whitaker mentioned an earlier conversation where "the Russians" told Special Presidential Envoy Steve Witkoff they were not "participating with the Iranians"

stories.title: Israel, US strike eastern Tehran — agency
stories.description: No reports about damage or casualties are available at this point

stories.title: European Council to discuss Iran, Ukraine on March 19 — Costa
stories.description: The situation in Lebanon, the Gaza Strip and the West Bank is also on the agenda

stories.title: Iraqi tankers can cross Strait of Hormuz — Iran’s ambassador to Baghdad
stories.description: Two tankers were attacked in the Iraqi waters a day before the statement

stories.title: US distorts facts claiming that Tehran enriched uranium to 60% — envoy to UN
stories.description: Amir-Saeid Iravani says Washington "ignores the real root of the situation"

stories.title: Iranian General Staff says US refueling aircraft was downed over western Iraq
stories.description: The Central Command of the US armed forces said that the plane had been lost not due to hostile fire or friendly fire

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Russian oil loaded on vessels before March 12 — Treasury Department
stories.description: The license allows transactions with Russian fuel before April 11

stories.title: Central Iranian city of Arak bombed — TV
stories.description: No information on damage or casualties is available so far

stories.title: Iran warns against attacks on its energy infrastructure
stories.description: A representative of Khatam al-Anbiya, the central headquarters of the Iranian army, says that otherwise Iran would destroy all oil and gas infrastructure of interest to the US in the region

stories.title: During talks, Iran proposed formally sealing its refusal from nuclear ambitions — minister
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi says Washington turned down the proposal
